title: [EPIC] kiro_cockpit — NanoPlanner + PuppySwarm (plan2.md)
description: Greenfield Elixir/Phoenix kiro_cockpit application implementing the full plan2.md vision: Part A NanoPlanner (approval-gated planning brain wrapping kiro-cli ACP) + Part B PuppySwarm (Code Puppy intelligence + Swarm SDK deterministic runtime with hooks/tasks/steering/Bronze→Silver→Gold memory). Source: plan2.md (95.9 KB, 22+ source phases collapsed into 5 strategic phases). Hard rules R1-R10 (§25.3) and Swarm invariants 1-10 (§27.11) must be enforced in code, not prompts. Critical correctness rule: ACP turn completion requires session/update turn_end, NEVER session/prompt response alone.

title: [PHASE 3] Swarm Runtime — Hooks, Tasks, Steering
description: Deterministic enforcement layer: every action passes a priority-ordered hook chain, every non-exempt action requires an active task with a category, plan mode is read-only, an LLM steering agent decides drift. Estimated 2.5 weeks. Acceptance: a non-exempt action without an active task is BLOCKED with actionable guidance; off-plan write in 'acting' mode is BLOCKED; steering FOCUSES drift and BLOCKS off-topic actions. Source: plan2.md §27.1-§27.9, §35 Phases 9-11+14, §36.1-§36.3.

title: [PHASE 2] NanoPlanner Brain
description: Read-only discovery → structured plan → user approval → generated Kiro execution prompt loop, with permission prediction, stale-plan detection, CLI + LiveView surfaces. Estimated 2 weeks. Acceptance: §20 MVP — /nano <task> → strong plan → approve → Kiro receives generated execution prompt → timeline events visible. Source: plan2.md §4-§22 (all of Part A except Phase 0 and Phase 8).

title: [PHASE 4] PuppyBrain Intelligence + Bronze→Silver→Gold Memory
description: Code-Puppy-style dynamic prompt assembly, two-pass MCP/tool composition, full data pipeline (Bronze raw events → Silver findings → Gold memories) so the planner gets smarter over time. Estimated 2.5–3 weeks. Acceptance: an anti-pattern learned in one session (e.g. ACP turn-completion rule) reappears as Gold memory guidance in a later NanoPlanner plan; local tools shadow MCP name conflicts. Source: plan2.md §26.1-§26.15, §28, §35 Phases 12-13, 15-19, §36.4-§36.6.

title: [PHASE 5] UI, Hardening, Loops, Release
description: Complete LiveView/CLI parity for Swarm internals, security hardening, audit trail, loop runner, ADRs, end-to-end regression suite, release prep. Estimated 1.5–2 weeks. Acceptance: §38 example flow runs end-to-end; all hard rules R1-R10 and invariants 1-10 are enforced in code (not just prompts). Source: plan2.md §18 Phase 8, §33, §35 Phases 20-21, §36.7-§36.8, §38.

title: [PHASE 1] ACP Foundation
description: Reliable Elixir ACP client: spawn kiro-cli, run a full turn, stream session/update events, service fs/* + terminal/* callbacks, persist raw events. Estimated 1.5–2 weeks. Acceptance: real kiro-cli acp round-trip works end-to-end and the fake-agent regression test for turn-end discipline passes. Source: plan2.md §3, §18 Phase 0, §20 items 1-2; kiro-acp-instructions.md.

title: Final review pass: security + Elixir + critic
ADRs: hook contract, Bronze→Silver→Gold, plan mode invariants, category matrix, MCP composition
Full audit trail surface (compliance audit hook + UI)
End-to-end regression suite (§36.7 UI + §36.8 e2e)
description: Agent: qa-expert. Critical: turn_end discipline, off-plan-write blocked, learned-memory reuse across sessions.

title: Prompt-injection protections; rules cannot disable wrapper safety policy
LiveView: SessionTasksLive, HookTraceLive, MemoryLive, AgentRegistryLive, ToolRegistryLive
LocalFindings + ToolResultAnalysis + WriteValidation + PostActing + TaskMaintenance hooks
Gold memory store + Retriever + Consolidator + DreamHook
PromptAssembler + RuleLoader + SkillRegistry + AgentRegistry
description: Files: lib/kiro_cockpit/puppy_brain/{prompt_assembler,rule_loader,skill_registry,agent_registry}.ex. Agents: elixir-programmer + agent-platform-architect. Notes: project rules cannot override hard policy invariants.

title: Code review + invariant audit for Phase 4
Two-pass tool composition (local probe → MCP filter)
Memory retrieval injected into NanoPlanner prompt assembly
Silver analyzers: default, error, insight, dream, steering
First wave of hooks: TaskEnforcement, PlanModeFirstAction, SteeringPreAction (deterministic), TaskGuidance
Code review + invariant audit for Phase 3
Steering agent (LLM-backed) + swarm_steering_prompt.md
description: Files: lib/kiro_cockpit/swarm/steering_agent.ex, priv/prompts/swarm_steering_prompt.md. Agents: prompt-reviewer + elixir-programmer. Validation: continue/focus/guide/block decisions, JSON-strict, fallback when model unavailable.

title: Permission matrix integration (§32) — wire to Phase 2 permissions
Event + Hook + HookManager + HookResult + TraceContext
description: Files: lib/kiro_cockpit/swarm/{event,hook,hook_manager,hook_result,trace_context}.ex. Agent: agent-platform-architect (owns hook contract). Validation: deterministic priority ordering, continue/block/modify.

title: Integration tests: fake-planner, LiveView mount/render, approval routing
Custom Kiro agent configs (planner read-only / executor write-able)
ContextBuilder + ProjectSnapshot (hashing, context budget)
description: Files: lib/kiro_cockpit/nano_planner/context_builder.ex, lib/kiro_cockpit/project_snapshot.ex. Agent: elixir-programmer. Notes: enforce read-only discovery, budget caps from §8.

title: Plan persistence: plans, plan_steps, plan_events tables
Client callbacks: fs/read_text_file, fs/write_text_file, terminal/*
description: Agent: elixir-programmer. Notes: paths MUST be absolute, lines 1-based.

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text: Shepherd REQUEST_CHANGES after hardening commit 966be76. Blocking gaps: known callbacks with non-map params can raise BadMapError/crash instead of -32602; terminal async handler must catch exits from TerminalManager/GenServer.call and still send best-effort JSON-RPC error; terminal output may include invalid UTF-8/truncation mid-codepoint causing Jason encode failure/drop.

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text: Shepherd REQUEST_CHANGES addressed in commit d2fc4d0. Local validation before critic rerun: mix format --check-formatted PASS, mix compile --warnings-as-errors PASS, focused callbacks/terminal/integration tests 81 PASS, full mix test 268 PASS, mix credo --strict PASS, git clean. Keep in_progress pending critics.

title: Scaffold mix project + supervision tree + Phoenix + Ecto/Postgres
description: Files: mix.exs, lib/kiro_cockpit/application.ex, config/*. Agent: elixir-programmer.
close_reason: Successfully merged into main via commit f572efb. Base validation passed: deps fetched, format check clean, compile warnings-as-errors clean, tests passing (1 test, 0 failures).

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text: Pack Leader dispatching to implementation worktree from main branch

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text:
🐕 SHEPHERD CRITIC — REQUEST_CHANGES

Key blockers identified:

1. **CRITICAL: Phoenix.LiveReloader.Socket child in Application causing dev startup crash**
   - Invalid child spec for LiveReloader.Socket in Application supervision tree
   - This breaks development environment startup

2. **SECURITY: dev/test secret_key_base too short**
   - Secret key base does not meet Phoenix minimum length requirements
   - Will cause runtime errors in dev/test environments

3. **CONFIG MISMATCH: Asset aliases vs config profiles**
   - Asset aliases use default Phoenix paths while config/profiles use :kiro_cockpit naming
   - Creates inconsistency between build-time and runtime asset handling

4. **PRODUCTION: SSL config unsafe/too opinionated**
   - Production runtime SSL configuration is overly prescriptive
   - May conflict with infrastructure-level SSL termination

5. **PRODUCTION: Missing conventional PHX_SERVER/cache_static_manifest config**
   - Standard Phoenix production environment variables not properly configured
   - Affects static asset serving and server behavior

6. **DEPENDENCIES: Over-scaffolded dependency set**
   - Contains unused dependencies for future features not yet implemented
   - Increases build time and attack surface unnecessarily

7. **RUNTIME: Avoid Mix.env in runtime/router where possible**
   - Mix.env should not be used at runtime (compile-time only)
   - Router contains Mix.env checks that should be refactored

comments.author: asx8678
comments.text:
🐕‍🦺 WATCHDOG CRITIC — REQUEST_CHANGES

Key blockers identified:

1. **CRITICAL: mix test fails due to test secret_key_base shorter than 64 bytes**
   - Phoenix requires secret_key_base minimum 64 bytes
   - This is NOT a Postgres limitation — it's a Phoenix security requirement
   - Test suite cannot run until fixed

2. **NOTED: Python pytest unavailable**
   - Python testing infrastructure not available during review
   - Python area was unchanged as part of this scaffold
   - NOT treated as scaffold failure since no changes were made to Python code
